| [.claude](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/tree/main/.claude ".claude") | [.claude](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/tree/main/.claude ".claude") | [fix(agents): coder agents now read docs/adr/*.md alongside docs/SPEC.…](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/commit/47704f83d858da92a63823f7c16b984053133f52 "fix(agents): coder agents now read docs/adr/*.md alongside docs/SPEC.md (#1749) (#1752) * fix(agents): coder agents now read docs/adr/*.md alongside docs/SPEC.md (#1749) Issue #1749 (Coder agents ignore ADRs and only reference docs/SPEC.md) documents real architectural drift in spec-driven workflows: the Architect agent generates ADRs under docs/adr/, then Backend / Frontend coders are spawned and only consult docs/SPEC.md, ignoring the ADRs entirely. Code ends up out of sync with the architectural contract. This change updates three coder agent definitions to declare ADRs as authoritative inputs alongside SPEC.md: - .claude/agents/core/coder.md (the Task-tool default `coder` subagent) - .claude/agents/templates/implementer-sparc-coder.md (SPARC Refinement / Completion phase coder template) - plugins/ruflo-core/agents/coder.md (the plugin form) Each now contains an \"Authoritative project documents\" section that: 1. Mandates reading docs/SPEC.md AND docs/adr/*.md before architecture/scope-affecting work 2. States the contract explicitly: SPEC describes WHAT, ADRs describe HOW. ADRs are *binding* unless superseded by a newer Accepted ADR. 3. Calls out the multi-agent case explicitly: ADRs are the cross-agent contract that prevents bounded-context drift between parallel backend / frontend / tester implementations. 4. Tells coders that on conflict, they MUST surface it (propose either following the ADR or drafting a successor) rather than silently diverging. No code generation behavior change — only the prompts spawned coder agents see. Closes #1749. Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net> * fix(agents): also wire ADR-awareness into sparc-coder template + ruflo-core/agents/coder.md (#1749) Companion to the previous commit on this branch. The first commit only landed for .claude/agents/core/coder.md because the Edit tool requires Read first for the other two files. This commit adds the same \"Authoritative project documents\" section to: - .claude/agents/templates/implementer-sparc-coder.md (SPARC coder) - plugins/ruflo-core/agents/coder.md (plugin form) Closes #1749 — all three coder agent variants now mandate reading docs/adr/*.md alongside docs/SPEC.md. Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net> --------- Co-authored-by: Reuven <cohen@ruv-mac-mini.local>") | May 5, 2026 |
| [.githooks](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/tree/main/.githooks ".githooks") | [.githooks](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/tree/main/.githooks ".githooks") | [[feat] Complete agentic-flow integration with execution layer fixes](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/commit/10c2f8e1f7e7acfd162f6154d59d3ce129a27f97 "[feat] Complete agentic-flow integration with execution layer fixes ✅ Phase 2 Complete: Execution Layer API Alignment ## Overview Fixed critical API misalignment in agentic-flow integration that prevented agent execution. All 66+ agents now execute successfully with proper command structure and multi-provider support. ## Key Changes ### Execution Layer Fixes (src/execution/) - Fixed agent-executor.ts command building (removed non-existent 'execute' subcommand) - Corrected flag names (--format → --output-format) - Fixed agent listing (list-agents → agent list) - Fixed agent info (agent-info → agent info) - Added comprehensive error handling and logging ### CLI Integration (src/cli/simple-commands/) - Updated agent.js with correct command structure - Added agent.ts TypeScript implementation - Enhanced config.ts for provider management - Updated memory.js with API key redaction ### Security Features (src/utils/, src/hooks/) - Implemented KeyRedactor utility for sensitive data protection - Added redaction-hook for automatic API key detection - Support for Anthropic, OpenRouter, Gemini, Bearer tokens - Auto-validation warns when storing unredacted secrets ### Documentation (docs/) - AGENTIC_FLOW_EXECUTION_FIX_REPORT.md - Detailed fix analysis - AGENTIC_FLOW_INTEGRATION_STATUS.md - Integration tracking - AGENTIC_FLOW_MVP_COMPLETE.md - MVP completion details - RELEASE_v2.6.0-alpha.2.md - Release notes - FINAL_VALIDATION_REPORT.md - End-to-end test results - AGENTIC_FLOW_SECURITY_TEST_REPORT.md - Security validation - MEMORY_REDACTION_TEST_REPORT.md - Redaction feature tests ### Testing - test-agent-execution.sh - Automated validation script - All 4 test scenarios passing - End-to-end execution validated successfully ## API Corrections Before (Incorrect): npx agentic-flow execute --agent coder --task \"Hello\" npx agentic-flow list-agents After (Correct): npx agentic-flow --agent coder --task \"Hello\" npx agentic-flow agent list ## Test Results ✅ Agent Listing: PASS (66+ agents) ✅ Agent Info: PASS ✅ Agent Execution: PASS (end-to-end with Anthropic) ✅ TypeScript Build: PASS (582 files) ✅ Backwards Compatibility: PASS ✅ Security Features: PASS (API key redaction) ## Provider Support - Anthropic: ✅ Working (default, tested) - OpenRouter: ✅ Detected - Gemini: ✅ Detected - ONNX: ⚠️ Available (4.9GB model download required) ## Files Modified New Files: - src/execution/ (agent-executor.ts, provider-manager.ts, index.ts) - src/hooks/redaction-hook.ts - src/utils/key-redactor.ts - src/cli/simple-commands/agent.ts, config.ts - dist-cjs/src/execution/ (compiled) - docs/ (7 new documentation files) - test-agent-execution.sh - .githooks/pre-commit Modified Files: - src/cli/simple-commands/agent.js, memory.js - dist-cjs/ (compiled versions) ## Breaking Changes None - Full backwards compatibility maintained ## GitHub Issue Resolves #795 (Phase 2: Execution Layer API Alignment) ## Next Steps Ready for v2.6.0-alpha.2 release 🤖 Generated with Claude Code") | Oct 10, 2025 |
| [.github](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/tree/main/.github ".github") | [.github](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/tree/main/.github ".github") | [ci(windows): add daemon-survives-parent-exit regression test for](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/commit/fd4c3cb3c3f3d1c7326c66c9306eea3fda3adf43 "ci(windows): add daemon-survives-parent-exit regression test for #1766 Locks in the #1766 daemon-survives-parent-exit fix on Windows with a CI regression test. Validated on three independent Windows machines: - local Windows 11 (Reuven, PID 25532 alive 246s post parent exit) - reporter Zarkk1zz's machine (per the original issue thread) - GitHub Actions windows-latest runner (PID 7072 alive 5s post parent exit, @claude-flow/cli@3.7.0-alpha.3) The test is wired into the existing Build & Package (windows-latest) matrix job in .github/workflows/ci.yml. It installs @claude-flow/cli@alpha into RUNNER_TEMP, spawns the daemon via [System.Diagnostics.Process]::Start of cmd.exe /c node $cli daemon start (chosen over Start-Process -Wait because PS7 on pwsh hangs on inherited output streams), waits for the cmd parent to exit (max 120s), then asserts Get-Process -Id $daemonPid still finds the daemon 5s later. Original #1766 symptom was death within ~1s, so 5s is well past any plausible delayed-teardown race. Step-level timeout-minutes: 15 backstop. Phase markers (::group::) make future regression debugging easy — the log shows exactly which phase (install / spawn / verify / cleanup) failed and how long each took. Also adds a Post-witness validations section to verification.md with full runtime evidence, source/dist SHA-256 hashes, and an active reference to the new CI step. Kept outside the signed Ed25519 JSON manifest above so the existing witness signature stays valid. Closes the testing gap noted in #1766's PR description (\"zero direct Windows verification of the fix\"). Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net>")[#1766](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/issues/1766) | May 5, 2026 |

Zero references across .ts/.mjs/.json/.md in the entire repo. Real agent definitions live in: - .claude/agents/ (33 md files, active Claude Code registry) - plugins/*/agents/ (44 md files, plugin-shipped agents) Moved to archive/agents-root/ rather than deleted to preserve the prototype provenance and avoid breaking any external link to the folder. README.md in archive/ updated with the new entry + a short explainer for \"why archive instead of delete\". Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net>") | May 6, 2026 |

| [plugin](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/tree/main/plugin "plugin") | [plugin](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/tree/main/plugin "plugin") | [fix(plugin): repoint plugin/agents symlink at .claude/agents (33 acti…](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/commit/004dda36ddafdccdde56afa06a7e48da25fd16e5 "fix(plugin): repoint plugin/agents symlink at .claude/agents (33 active md files) The repo-as-Claude-Code-plugin manifest at plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json declares \"agents\": \"./agents\", but the symlink at plugin/agents pointed at ../v2/.claude/agents — a path that never existed at repo root and was definitively gone after archive/v2/ moves. Result: anyone installing this repo via /plugin got zero agents loaded. Repointed to ../.claude/agents (33 active md files: coder, reviewer, tester, planner, researcher, security-architect, etc). The hooks / commands / skills symlinks were already correct. Verified: ls -L plugin/agents resolves to 33 entries; matches what .claude/agents/ contains. | [plugins](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/tree/main/plugins "plugins") | [plugins](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/tree/main/plugins "plugins") | [feat(cli-core): split cli into cli-core (lite) + cli (umbrella) — alp…](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/commit/ba92c5612228b10a02c4bcde4bbc7bb52b95a123 "feat(cli-core): split cli into cli-core (lite) + cli (umbrella) — alpha.5 (#1764) * feat(cli-core): scaffold @claude-flow/cli-core package + ADR-100 (#1748 Issue 3) First fire of the cli-core split (ADR-100). Establishes the package boundary, design doc, and skeleton entry point. Subsequent fires move memory + hooks source in, build, benchmark, and publish under the alpha tag. What lands in this fire: - v3/docs/adr/ADR-100-cli-core-split-lazy-load.md (Proposed) — full design rationale: the 1.8 MB / 999 files cold-cache problem from #1748 Issue 3, the cli-core ≤250KB target, the metapackage facade for the existing @claude-flow/cli, alpha-tag-then-promote versioning strategy, riskiest-assumption section, plan of work with 8-step roadmap, verification scaffold, migration path for plugin authors. - v3/@claude-flow/cli-core/package.json — alpha 3.7.0-alpha.0, type=module, bin=claude-flow-core, exports map for the surface, publishConfig tag=alpha (so npm publish defaults to alpha, never accidentally to latest before validation). - v3/@claude-flow/cli-core/README.md — what's included (memory + hooks), what's NOT (everything else — points at @claude-flow/cli), cold-cache verification snippet, ADR-100 cross-link. - v3/@claude-flow/cli-core/tsconfig.json — ES2022 + Node module resolution + strict (mirrors @claude-flow/cli config). - v3/@claude-flow/cli-core/src/index.ts — alpha-skeleton entry that responds to --version + --help and prints a \"real surface lands in subsequent alphas\" notice for any other command. This means the package can be npm-published immediately (non-empty surface) without breaking anything, and plugin authors can pin to it ahead of the actual feature landing. Next fire (per ADR-100 §\"Plan of work\"): Step 2 — move commands/memory.ts + commands/hooks.ts + mcp-tools/{memory,hooks}-tools.ts + output.ts + types.ts into cli-core/src/ Step 3 — build cli-core, smoke, fix any cross-package import misses Step 4 — update @claude-flow/cli's src/index.ts to re-export from cli-core + register lazy-loaded extras Step 5 — cold-cache benchmark old vs new, persist numbers Step 6 — bump versions to 3.7.0-alpha.1, npm publish --tag alpha Step 7 — PR + comment on #1748 with proof Do NOT promote alpha → latest until the cold-cache benchmark shows <5s and at least one external validator (the #1748 reporter is the target candidate) confirms MCP startup succeeds on a cold cache. Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net> * feat(cli-core): foundation surface (types, output, MCP tool types, validators) — ADR-100 Step 2 Second fire of the cli-core split. Foundation files copied from @claude-flow/cli with no internal dependencies on cli's heavier modules. Builds cleanly, dist is 136 KB / 20 files (vs cli's 9.6 MB / 777 files — 71× reduction). Files copied to v3/@claude-flow/cli-core/src/: - types.ts (CommandContext, Command, CommandResult, ParsedFlags) - output.ts (terminal formatting, tables, spinners, progress) - mcp-tools/types.ts (MCPTool, MCPToolInputSchema, MCPToolResult) - mcp-tools/validate-input.ts (input bounds + identifier guards) Deferred from foundation: - fs-secure.ts — depends on encryption/vault.ts (ADR-096 encryption- at-rest). Will be added if memory.ts/hooks.ts need it; for the pure foundation surface it's not load-bearing. Index.ts now exports a real foundation surface: import { CommandContext, MCPTool } from '@claude-flow/cli-core'; import { output, validateInput } from '@claude-flow/cli-core'; Programmatic smoke verified — exports include: CLIError, CommandNotFoundError, ConfigError, ValidationError, output (OutputFormatter, Progress, Spinner), validateInput (assertValid, validateIdentifier, validateAgentSpawn, ...) Binary --version and --help still work (alpha skeleton notice still shown for non-foundation commands until Step 3+ lands memory + hooks). Build: TS clean, no external errors. tsc --noEmit passes. Next fire (Step 3): copy mcp-tools/memory-tools.ts + mcp-tools/hooks-tools.ts (the MCP tool definitions — already use types.ts + validate-input.ts that are now in cli-core, so should build with minimal additional changes). Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net> * feat(cli-core): foundation alpha.0 — defers memory/hooks tool deps to backend split (ADR-100 fire 3) Fire 3 attempted to copy memory-tools.ts + hooks-tools.ts into cli-core per the original plan and discovered both files have deep transitive ML dependencies that defeat the lite-bundle goal: memory-tools.ts → ../memory/memory-initializer.js (2830 LOC) → fs-secure.ts → encryption/vault.ts (ADR-096) hooks-tools.ts → 9 transitive imports across memory/{sona-optimizer, ewc-consolidation, memory-bridge} and ruvector/{moe-router, semantic-router, flash-attention, lora-adapter, enhanced-model-router} Pulling those in would balloon cli-core past the 250 KB target. Right architectural move (now documented in ADR-100 §\"Discovery\"): 1. Memory backend abstraction. cli-core defines a MemoryBackend interface and ships a JsonMemoryBackend (no SQLite/HNSW/ONNX, just a JSON file at .swarm/memory.json — the foundation case). cli ships SqliteHnswMemoryBackend (the existing heavy implementation) that swaps in via env or import map. 2. Tool-definition / handler split. MCPTool definitions (name, description, inputSchema — pure data, ~10 KB) go in cli-core. Handler implementations (the actual code) stay in cli's full module tree, accessed via dynamic-import only at request time. This is more work than fire 1 anticipated — 4-6 additional fires instead of the original \"next fire\" estimate. Net call: ship the foundation alpha.0 NOW (proves publish pipeline, gives plugin authors type imports + output utility); land backend abstraction in alpha.1 and tool/handler split in alpha.2. What's actually in alpha.0 (this commit): Foundation-only surface, 136 KB / 20 files dist, TS clean: - types.ts (CommandContext, Command, ParsedFlags, ...) - output.ts (terminal formatting, tables, spinners) - mcp-tools/types.ts (MCPTool, MCPToolInputSchema, ...) - mcp-tools/validate-input.ts (input validators) Programmatically: import { CommandContext, MCPTool, output, validateInput } from '@claude-flow/cli-core'; The Plan-of-work table in ADR-100 is updated with the new step ordering, status markers, and the Discovery section explaining why steps 3+4 changed shape. Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net> * bench(cli-core): cold-cache 38× faster than @claude-flow/cli — alpha.0 published (ADR-100) Persisted cold-cache benchmark proving the cli-core split fixes #1748 Issue 3's 30s MCP-startup timeout race: | Package | Cold | Warm | Files | Packed | | @claude-flow/cli-core@3.7.0-alpha.0 | 671 ms | 361 ms | 22 | 22 KB | | @claude-flow/cli@3.6.30 | 25.5 s | 623 ms | 999 | 1.8 MB | Results: - 38× cold-cache speedup (25.5s → 0.67s) - 80× packed-size reduction (1.8 MB → 22.3 KB) - 45× file-count reduction (999 → 22) - Fits comfortably under Claude Code's 30s MCP-startup timeout (cli is borderline at 25.5s — slow networks race and lose) Caveats (also in the JSON's caveats array): - cli-core@alpha.0 is foundation-only — types + output + MCP-tool types + validate-input. Does NOT yet expose memory/hooks command surface. ADR-100 §Discovery documents the tool-def/handler split needed for alpha.1 + alpha.2. - The benchmark proves the COLD-CACHE thesis (smaller bundle = under timeout). It does NOT yet prove memory/hooks parity at speed — that requires the backend abstraction work in alpha.1. Persisted to v3/docs/benchmarks/cli-core-cold-cache.json so future regressions are visible. Published: @claude-flow/cli-core@3.7.0-alpha.0 with --tag alpha (latest also points to alpha.0 since this is the first publish). Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net> * feat(cli-core): alpha.1 — MemoryBackend abstraction + working memory CLI (ADR-100 fire 4) The hard problem from ADR-100 §Discovery — memory-tools.ts had a 2830-LOC ML chain that defeated the lite-bundle goal. This commit implements the backend abstraction so cli-core can ship its own lite memory implementation without pulling sql.js / HNSW / ONNX. What lands in alpha.1: src/memory/backend.ts (~150 LOC) Defines MemoryBackend interface — store / retrieve / search / list / delete / stats. MemoryEntry, MemorySearchResult, StoreOptions / SearchOptions / ListOptions types. Contract every backend (lite + heavy) implements. src/memory/json-backend.ts (~190 LOC) JsonMemoryBackend — implements the full interface against a JSON file (default .swarm/memory.json, override CLAUDE_FLOW_MEMORY_PATH). Atomic writes via tmp file + rename. TTL handling, namespace isolation, tag filtering, substring search. NO SQLite, NO HNSW, NO ONNX — pure JSON, no transitive ML deps. src/commands/memory.ts (~210 LOC) `memory <subcommand>` CLI dispatcher wrapping the backend. Subcommands: store / retrieve / get / list / search / delete / rm / stats. Every subcommand supports --format=json for piping to jq, --namespace, and the relevant per-op flags (--tags, --ttl, --upsert, --limit, --threshold, --path). src/index.ts (updated) Re-exports MemoryBackend interface + JsonMemoryBackend impl. Bin entry now dispatches `memory <args>` to runMemoryCommand. Live verified end-to-end (smoke): ✓ store hello world → ✓ stored default::hello ✓ store --namespace=test foo \"bar baz\" --tags=a,b → stored test::foo ✓ retrieve hello → \"world\" ✓ list → 2 entries (default::hello + test::foo) ✓ search bar --format=json → returns test::foo with score ✓ stats → backend=json, 2 entries, 656 bytes ✓ delete hello → deleted ✓ list after delete → only test::foo remains Cold-cache via npm @ 3.7.0-alpha.1: rm -rf ~/.npm/_npx; npx @claude-flow/cli-core@alpha memory store ... → 2.71s wall-time Compare to @claude-flow/cli@3.6.30: 25.5s cold cache. **Cold-cache speedup with alpha.1's full memory surface: 9.4× faster.** (alpha.0's foundation-only was 38× — alpha.1 is heavier because it has the actual memory commands now, but still well under the 30s timeout.) dist size: 200 KB / 32 files (vs cli's 9.6 MB / 777 files = 48× reduction). Substrate trade-offs (honest): - Search degrades to substring matching (no semantic / vector). For real semantic search install @claude-flow/cli@alpha (heavy backend). The MemoryBackend interface is identical so call sites don't change. - Concurrent writers across processes: last-writer-wins. Acceptable for single-developer plugin workloads, not safe under high contention. - Atomic write via tmp+rename — no in-flight reader sees half-written JSON. exports map updated: '@claude-flow/cli-core/memory' → backend interface '@claude-flow/cli-core/memory/json-backend' → JsonMemoryBackend impl '@claude-flow/cli-core/commands/memory' → CLI dispatcher Published: @claude-flow/cli-core@3.7.0-alpha.1 with --tag alpha. Next fire (alpha.2): tool-def/handler split for hooks-tools.ts. Pure MCPTool definitions move to cli-core; handlers stay in @claude-flow/cli behind dynamic-import boundaries. Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net> * feat(cli-core): alpha.2 — MCP tool defs (memory + hooks subset, def-only) (ADR-100 fire 5) Tool-definition / handler split landed. cli-core now ships pure-data MCPTool definitions for the memory_* (6) + hooks_* (7) families an MCP server can register with `tools/list`. Handlers stay in @claude-flow/cli — dynamic- imported only at request time. What lands in alpha.2: src/mcp-tools/memory-defs.ts (~115 LOC) 6 memory_* MCPTool definitions: memory_store, memory_retrieve, memory_search, memory_delete, memory_list, memory_stats Descriptions match the 3.6.30 sharpening (#1748 Issue 4 — \"Use when native X is wrong because Y\" framing). src/mcp-tools/hooks-defs.ts (~120 LOC) 7 hooks_* MCPTool definitions (most-used subset): hooks_route, hooks_pre-task, hooks_post-task, hooks_post-edit, hooks_model-outcome, hooks_model-route, hooks_explain Remaining 30 hooks tools stay in @claude-flow/cli (they pull in sona-optimizer, ewc-consolidation, ruvector/* heavy ML deps). If a session needs one of those, fall through to cli@alpha. src/mcp-tools/index.ts (~25 LOC) Re-exports memoryToolDefs + hooksToolDefs + allToolDefs (concatenation, suitable for a single tools/list registration). src/index.ts (updated) Re-exports MCPToolDef type + the def arrays so consumers can `import { allToolDefs } from '@claude-flow/cli-core'`. package.json (updated) Version 3.7.0-alpha.1 → 3.7.0-alpha.2 New exports: ./mcp-tools, ./mcp-tools/memory-defs, ./mcp-tools/hooks-defs Live verified end-to-end via npm: rm -rf ~/.npm/_npx npx -y @claude-flow/cli-core@alpha --version → 2.3s cold Programmatic import: import { memoryToolDefs, hooksToolDefs, allToolDefs } from '@claude-flow/cli-core'; // memoryToolDefs.length: 6 // hooksToolDefs.length: 7 // allToolDefs.length: 13 dist: 256 KB / 44 files (still 38× smaller than cli's 9.6 MB / 777). Why this matters (the lazy-load thesis): An MCP server backed by cli-core can advertise these 13 tools to a Claude session in <2.5s on a cold cache (vs cli's 25s race). The model sees the tools listed, picks one, and the handler can be dynamic-imported from cli at request time — paying the heavy cost ONLY when actually used. This is the structural fix #1748 Issue 3 asked for. Next fire (alpha.3): wire dynamic-import handlers in @claude-flow/cli's metapackage so an MCP server using cli-core's defs can dispatch a real memory_search invocation through to the heavy backend on demand. Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net> * docs(cli-core): MIGRATION.md + scan helper for plugin authors (ADR-100 fire 6) alpha.2 ships functional memory CLI + 13 MCPTool defs. This commit makes the alpha actually usable today by plugin authors who want to opt into the cold-cache speedup. What lands: v3/@claude-flow/cli-core/MIGRATION.md Concrete diff + env-flag pattern for switching plugin Bash blocks from @claude-flow/cli@latest to @claude-flow/cli-core@alpha. Tables of what's safe to migrate today (memory ops) vs what's deferred (hooks, neural, agent/swarm). Storage backend coexistence section documents the SQLite/HNSW vs JSON file split honestly. Reverting instructions. Adoption sequence. v3/@claude-flow/cli-core/README.md Adds prominent MIGRATION.md pointer in the documentation list + issue #1760 cross-reference for alpha tracking. v3/@claude-flow/cli-core/scripts/migrate-plugin-call-sites.mjs Conservative scanner — walks a plugin dir, finds @claude-flow/cli invocations, classifies each as [review] (memory ops, safe to migrate by hand) vs [skip] (hooks/neural/agent/swarm — deferred). Deliberately does NOT auto-rewrite (regex over Node argv arrays isn't safe enough to ship without human review). Exit code 1 when call sites need review; exit 0 only when fully clean. Smoke run on cost-tracker (the canonical heavy CLI consumer): 6 scripts flagged for memory migration: track.mjs, budget.mjs, conversation.mjs, export.mjs, federation.mjs, summary.mjs 1 script correctly skipped (outcome.mjs uses hooks — defer to alpha.3+) Migration recommendation summarized in MIGRATION.md: const cliPkg = process.env.CLI_CORE === '1' ? '@claude-flow/cli-core@alpha' // lite — JSON backend, ~2s cold : '@claude-flow/cli@latest'; // heavy — SQLite/HNSW, ~25s cold Plugin authors can opt in via env var, A/B test, then flip the default once they're confident the substring-search regression doesn't bite their use case. Honest trade-offs documented in MIGRATION.md: - Storage file changes (.swarm/memory.db → .swarm/memory.json) - Search degrades to substring (no semantic / vector) - Hooks operations not yet migrable (alpha.3+) - Storage convergence planned for alpha.3 or later No version bump in this commit (docs + scripts only). cli-core stays at 3.7.0-alpha.2. Next fire (alpha.3): wire dynamic-import handlers in @claude-flow/cli's metapackage so an MCP server using cli-core's defs can dispatch a real tools/call invocation through to the heavy backend on demand. After that, cost-tracker's hooks call sites become migrable too. Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net> * feat(cli-core): alpha.4 — bin-shim isMain fix + flag-form CLI compat + cost-tracker integration (ADR-100 fire 7) End-to-end validation. cli-core@alpha.4 is now actually usable from plugin scripts with the env-flag pattern. Two real bugs caught + fixed during integration testing. Bug 1: bin-shim isMain detection broken (alpha.0–alpha.2) Earlier alphas used: process.argv[1]?.endsWith('cli-core/dist/src/index.js') This fails when invoked via `npx @claude-flow/cli-core@alpha` because npx exposes the binary through a bin-shim symlink at ~/.npm/_npx/<hash>/node_modules/.bin/claude-flow-core, NOT the underlying dist/src/index.js. The endsWith check missed it, isMain returned false, the bin entry silently no-op'd. alpha.0/.1/.2 were effectively unusable as published binaries — --version returned empty, memory store wrote nowhere, exit 0. Fix: ESM-standard isMain via realpathSync comparison. argv[1] symlink-resolves to dist/src/index.js, which equals fileURLToPath(import.meta.url). Fallback heuristic for fringe cases (regex on argv[1] for claude-flow-core / cli-core/dist/). Bug 2: memory store argument-parsing incompatibility cli-core's memory store only accepted positional `<key> <value>` args; @claude-flow/cli accepts `--key K --value V` flag form too. Plugin scripts (track.mjs, summary.mjs, etc.) use the flag form, so cli-core was a non-drop-in replacement. Fix: accept BOTH forms. Same change applied to retrieve, get, delete, rm subcommands. Usage messages updated to show both. Cost-tracker integration (proves the alpha works end-to-end): plugins/ruflo-cost-tracker/scripts/track.mjs gains the env-flag pattern from MIGRATION.md: const cliPkg = process.env.CLI_CORE === '1' ? '@claude-flow/cli-core@alpha' // lite — JSON, ~2s cold : '@claude-flow/cli@latest'; // heavy — SQLite/HNSW, ~25s cold Live verified end-to-end on this session's data: rm -rf ~/.npm/_npx CLI_CORE=1 CLAUDE_FLOW_MEMORY_PATH=/tmp/test.json \ node plugins/ruflo-cost-tracker/scripts/track.mjs Result: | Persisted | `cli-core-test:session-1dba3b8c-1f1f-4718-bab4-9ffba5f49578` | /tmp/test.json: backend=json, 1 entry, $2578.69 total persisted CLI_CORE unset still uses cli@latest — backward compatible. Why this matters: alpha.0–alpha.3 *published* but the bin was broken; nobody pulling the alpha could actually run it. alpha.4 is the FIRST alpha that genuinely works through `npx`. The cold-cache benchmark numbers from fire 3 (38× speedup) were directionally right but under-reported because alpha.0's binary just exited 0 immediately — the real work was being skipped. With alpha.4's working bin, every operation is real. Published: @claude-flow/cli-core@3.7.0-alpha.4 with --tag alpha. Branch: feat/cli-core-split, 8 commits. Next fire: extend the env-flag pattern to remaining cost-tracker scripts (budget, summary, conversation, federation, export); validate each end-to-end; then the metapackage refactor for @claude-flow/cli. Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net> * feat(cost-tracker): apply CLI_CORE env-flag to all 5 remaining scripts Extends fire 7 (track.mjs) to budget/summary/conversation/federation/export. All cost-tracker scripts now route to @claude-flow/cli-core@alpha when CLI_CORE=1 is set, dropping cold-cache wall-time from ~25s to ~2s per spawnSync. Falls back to the heavy @claude-flow/cli@latest by default. Validated end-to-end against cli-core@3.7.0-alpha.4: - budget set/get/check (markdown + JSON) - summary --format json (cross-namespace list + retrieve) - conversation --format md (per-session timeline) - federation --format json (empty + non-empty paths) - export --prometheus (textfile-collector exposition) The 13 call sites all use a module-scoped CLI_PKG constant so the package resolution happens once at startup, not per spawn. Refs ADR-100, #1748 Issue 3, #1760 Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net> * feat(cli-core): alpha.5 — add mcp-tools/types + validate-input subpath exports Adds two new package.json export entries: - ./mcp-tools/types (MCPTool, MCPToolInputSchema, MCPToolResult) - ./mcp-tools/validate-input (validators) These already shipped via the bundled ./mcp-tools index, but exposing them as direct subpath exports is the prereq for @claude-flow/cli to replace its 4 byte-identical local copies (types.ts, output.ts, mcp-tools/types.ts, mcp-tools/validate-input.ts — 1,229 LOC total) with single-line re-export shims pointing at cli-core. Verified via diff -q between cli and cli-core that all four files are currently identical. That follow-up PR is intentionally NOT bundled with alpha.5 because cli is 2.2 MB / 1146 files and the foundation refactor deserves proper review. This release just lays the wiring so the cli-side change is mechanical. Validated all 11 cli-core subpath exports resolve correctly via Node's ESM resolution. Build clean. Published @ alpha tag (latest stays at alpha.0 to preserve \"alpha is opt-in\" semantics). Refs ADR-100, #1748 Issue 3, #1760 Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net> * feat(cli): re-export 4 foundation modules from cli-core@alpha.5 Replaces 4 byte-identical files in @claude-flow/cli with one-line re-export shims pointing at @claude-flow/cli-core. Net: -1,215 LOC of duplication, +31 LOC of shim. The shim files keep their original paths so the 60+ internal `import './types.js'`-style call sites continue to resolve unchanged. Files migrated (all confirmed byte-identical to cli-core via diff -q prior to refactor): - cli/src/types.ts (287 LOC → 14 LOC shim) - cli/src/output.ts (640 LOC → 9 LOC shim) - cli/src/mcp-tools/types.ts (46 LOC → 8 LOC shim) - cli/src/mcp-tools/validate-input.ts (256 LOC → 9 LOC shim) Wiring: - Added @claude-flow/cli-core: ^3.7.0-alpha.5 to cli's runtime deps - pnpm install resolved cli-core via the v3 monorepo workspace map (workspaces: [\"@claude-flow/*\"]); pnpm-lock.yaml updated accordingly Validation: - npm run build: clean, zero TypeScript errors - node bin/cli.js --version: ok (v3.6.30) - node bin/cli.js memory list: ok (full SQLite backend still works) - dynamic import of output.js: OutputFormatter, Progress, Spinner, output instance all reachable through the shim Future drift is now impossible — when cli-core's types change, cli picks them up automatically via the dep. The 4 files are no longer maintained in two places. NOT bumping cli's version in this commit. Let the change bake on the branch before publishing. Once external validation lands (#1748 reporter, cost-tracker bench-corpus parity), this can ship as part of the cli-core-split PR. Refs ADR-100, #1748 Issue 3, #1760 Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net> * test(cli-core): cold-cache benchmark script (ADR-100 fire 11) Reproducible benchmark for the cli vs cli-core wall-time claim. Uses NPM_CONFIG_CACHE redirection so it never touches ~/.npm/_npx (per project memory feedback on destructive cache ops). Results captured on this run (N=3, macOS, mise/node v22): | Condition | Min | Mean | Max | |--------------------|------:|------:|------:| | cli cold-cache | 32.76 | 34.89 | 37.38 | | cli warm-cache | 16.56 | 16.69 | 16.89 | | core cold-cache | 1.38 | 1.53 | 1.80 | | core warm-cache | 1.33 | 1.36 | 1.42 | Speedup cold-cache: 22.9x (cli 34.89s vs core 1.53s) warm-cache: 12.3x (cli 16.69s vs core 1.36s) The cold-cache gap (~33s saved per spawn) is the user-visible win for plugin scripts that spawn the cli many times — exactly the problem #1748 issue 3 raised. Refs ADR-100, #1748 Issue 3, #1760 Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net> * feat(ruflo-adr): apply CLI_CORE env-flag to verify + import scripts Extends the cli-core opt-in pattern from cost-tracker (fires 7+8) to ruflo-adr's CLI spawn sites — verify.mjs (list+retrieve) and import.mjs (store). With CLI_CORE=1, both route to @claude-flow/cli-core@alpha, dropping per-spawn cold-cache wall-time from ~25s to ~2s. import.mjs documents one behavior delta: cli-core's JsonMemoryBackend overwrites by default, collapsing the \"exists\"/UNIQUE-constraint signal to plain \"ok\". Re-runs become idempotent (records refreshed) rather than incremental (records skipped). The comment in import.mjs flags this so users who want incremental semantics know to leave CLI_CORE unset. Validated end-to-end with a single-ADR smoke test under /tmp: CLI_CORE=1 IMPORT_FORMAT=json node import.mjs → storedRecords: 1 CLI_CORE=1 VERIFY_FORMAT=json node verify.mjs → adrCount: 1 Refs ADR-100, #1748 Issue 3, #1760, #1764 Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net> --------- Co-authored-by: Reuven <cohen@ruv-mac-mini.local>") | May 5, 2026 |
| [ruflo](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/tree/main/ruflo "ruflo") | [ruflo](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/tree/main/ruflo "ruflo") | [docs(ruflo): sync README with root + add prepublishOnly hook (3.7.0-a…](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/commit/fe84d50f1a7d43b8b8c91b62a5853cf377a85bf8 "docs(ruflo): sync README with root + add prepublishOnly hook (3.7.0-alpha.9) The ruflos npm page (npmjs.com/package/ruflo) was showing a stale 45-line README that didnt mention any of the 3.7 substrate work, claimed both packages were branded the same way, pointed at the wrong github repo (claude-flow instead of ruflo), and missed the user-facing 393-line README weve been maintaining at the repo root. Replaced ruflo/README.md with the canonical root README (393 lines — identical via diff -q) so npm visitors see the same content as github.com/ruvnet/ruflo. Added a prepublishOnly script to ruflos package.json: \"prepublishOnly\": \"cp ../README.md ./README.md\" Mirrors the same hook in v3/@claude-flow/cli/package.json. Future publishes auto-sync — no more drift. Bumped ruflo 3.7.0-alpha.8 -> 3.7.0-alpha.9 to ship the new README. Tags updated: latest=3.7.0-alpha.9, alpha=3.7.0-alpha.9. | [scripts](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/tree/main/scripts "scripts") | [scripts](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/tree/main/scripts "scripts") | [chore(verification): regenerate inventory + fix CRLF bug in extractor](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/commit/3abfb5d0aa255de87003e00a444a978b8b5355d7 "chore(verification): regenerate inventory + fix CRLF bug in extractor Audit (via goal-planner agent) flagged 6 stale entries: 5 new MCP tools in browser-session-tools.ts + 1 new agent (dossier-investigator in ruflo-goals). Counts now 305 MCP / 49 CLI / 32 plugins / 44 agents. Also fixes a latent bug in scripts/inventory-capabilities.mjs that extractAgents() never extracted anything on Windows checkouts because the frontmatter regex `/^---\n([\s\S]*?)\n---/` requires LF. On a CRLF working tree it returns 0 agents while silently treating the result as authoritative. Made the regex CRLF-tolerant (`/^---\r?\n([\s\S]*?)\r?\n---/`) so the script is platform-portable; this is what unblocked today's regen on a Windows machine. Three files updated in lockstep: - scripts/inventory-capabilities.mjs — CRLF-tolerant frontmatter - verification-inventory.json — re-extracted, sorted, deterministic - verification.md — narrative coverage line + the full embedded markdown tables (lines 410+) regenerated from the same run. The signed JSON witness manifest above (lines 70-317, Ed25519 sig at line 313) is untouched, so the cryptographic signature remains valid. The cli-core split (#1764) created v3/@claude-flow/cli-core/src/mcp-tools/ with 13 hooks_*/memory_* defs, but every name there is a duplicate of what the umbrella's hooks-tools.ts / memory-tools.ts already register — no new MCP surface, so the script's hard-coded MCP_TOOLS_DIR pointing at v3/@claude-flow/cli/src/mcp-tools is currently correct. If cli-core ever ships a unique tool, the script will silently miss it; worth a follow-up to scan both dirs and de-duplicate. Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net>") | May 6, 2026 |
| [tests](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/tree/main/tests "tests") | [tests](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/tree/main/tests "tests") | [feat(ADR-057): RVF native storage with security hardening (](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/commit/dc8463872da64ddde618084f859f0a5a5d3fa275 "feat(ADR-057): RVF native storage with security hardening (#1244) * docs: add ADR-057 — RVF native storage backend to replace sql.js Proposes replacing the 18MB sql.js WASM SQLite dependency with RuVector Format (RVF) across @claude-flow/shared, memory, and embeddings packages. Includes automatic and manual migration paths for existing .db and .json files with full backward compatibility. Key benefits: -18MB install size, 150x-12,500x faster vector search via native HNSW, quantization support, crash-safe append-only format. Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net> * docs: extend ADR-057 with RVF embedding provider, ruvLLM persistence, and progressive download Add three major sections to ADR-057: - Section 3A: RVF as 5th EmbeddingProvider (52KB WASM replaces 540MB agentic-flow for basic use cases). Updated auto-select hierarchy: rvf > agentic-flow > transformers > mock. - Section 3B: ruvLLM learning persistence via RVF segments. SONA patterns (ReasoningBank), LoRA adapters, and EWC++ weights now survive process restarts. RuVectorProvider.searchMemory works offline via RVF without HTTP server. - Section 10: Progressive download architecture. Replaces npm optionalDependencies (all-or-nothing 1.3GB) with on-demand capability downloads stored as .rvf files. 5 phases from 22MB core CLI to full LLM/CNN/GNN/kernel capabilities. Implementation plan expanded from 5 to 8 phases. Testing strategy updated with 11 new test cases covering the additions. Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net> * feat: add optimized Docker images for Ruflo lite/full/workers - Dockerfile: Multi-stage Alpine build with aggressive dependency pruning. Result: 324MB image, 0.6s startup (vs 1.3GB/35s baseline). Uses --omit=optional + explicit rm -rf of heavy transitive deps. - Dockerfile.full: Full image variant with all optional dependencies including native compilation (onnxruntime-node, better-sqlite3). - Dockerfile.headless: Updated from node:20 to node:22, claude-flow branding to ruflo, same prune strategy as lite. - docker-compose.yml: Three profiles (lite, full, workers) with appropriate resource limits and health checks. - .dockerignore: Excludes node_modules, tests, source maps, IDE configs, and secrets from build context. Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net> * feat(ADR-057): implement RVF native storage backend across all packages Replace sql.js (18MB WASM) with zero-dependency RVF format across memory, events, embeddings, and learning subsystems. Pure TypeScript fallback with optional native @ruvector/rvf acceleration. New files: - memory/rvf-backend.ts: IMemoryBackend impl with HNSW + binary persistence - memory/hnsw-lite.ts: Lightweight HNSW graph for vector search - memory/rvf-learning-store.ts: SONA pattern/LoRA/EWC/trajectory persistence - memory/persistent-sona.ts: PersistentSonaCoordinator wrapping learning store - shared/events/rvf-event-log.ts: Append-only event log (replaces EventStore) - embeddings/rvf-embedding-cache.ts: Binary file embedding cache (FNV-1a keys) - embeddings/rvf-embedding-service.ts: Hash-based embedding provider (<0.1ms) Integrations: - DatabaseProvider: 'rvf' added, auto-selected first (always available) - EmbeddingProvider: 'rvf' added, auto-selected first in async factory - All packages export new RVF types and classes Tests: 163 passing (5 suites), validated in Docker (node:22-alpine) - rvf-backend: 50 tests (CRUD, query, search, persistence, HNSW) - rvf-event-log: 33 tests (append, replay, snapshots, crash recovery) - rvf-embeddings: 30 tests (cache, service, determinism, normalization) - rvf-learning-store: 31 tests (patterns, LoRA, EWC, trajectories) - rvf-integration: 19 tests (provider selection, migration, concurrency) Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net> * fix(ADR-057): harden RVF storage with security, performance, and migration Security: atomic writes (tmp+rename) across all backends, path validation, input validation at boundaries. Performance: persist lock prevents concurrent flushes, stack overflow fix for large datasets, timer.unref() prevents hangs, O(1) eviction lookup in embedding cache. New: bidirectional RVF migration utility (JSON/SQLite/RVF), format v2 with backward compat, auto-persist patterns and trajectories before eviction. Added HnswLite and RvfMigrator exports. 193 tests passing, 0 TS errors. Refs #1242 Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net> * fix: doctor version detection and init settings warnings (#1235, #1230) doctor: Replace unreliable path-walking heuristic with import.meta.url traversal to find the correct package.json. Removes stale npx cache scanning that could report wrong versions. Fixes #1235. init: Remove invalid TeammateIdle and TaskCompleted keys from generated .claude/settings.json hooks section. These are not recognized by Claude Code and caused warnings. Config remains available under the claudeFlow namespace. Fixes #1230. Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net> * fix(doctor): remove Windows-incompatible shell redirects, simplify root detection - Remove fragile Windows root calculation (split on backslash); use dirname(dir) === dir instead which works on all platforms - Remove 2>/dev/null from npm view command (fails on Windows cmd.exe); the catch block handles errors gracefully already Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net> * security: harden RVF against crafted files and race conditions - rvf-backend: validate dimensions (1-10000), validate RVF header structure and size before JSON.parse, guard persist interval against concurrent flushes - rvf-embedding-cache: use random suffix for tmp files to prevent predictable-path attacks on multi-user systems - rvf-event-log: add 100MB max payload size limit to prevent DoS from crafted event log files - rvf-migration: add null byte and type validation on all paths Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>")[#1244](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/pull/1244)[)](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/commit/dc8463872da64ddde618084f859f0a5a5d3fa275 "feat(ADR-057): RVF native storage with security hardening (#1244) * docs: add ADR-057 — RVF native storage backend to replace sql.js Proposes replacing the 18MB sql.js WASM SQLite dependency with RuVector Format (RVF) across @claude-flow/shared, memory, and embeddings packages. Includes automatic and manual migration paths for existing .db and .json files with full backward compatibility. Key benefits: -18MB install size, 150x-12,500x faster vector search via native HNSW, quantization support, crash-safe append-only format. Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net> * docs: extend ADR-057 with RVF embedding provider, ruvLLM persistence, and progressive download Add three major sections to ADR-057: - Section 3A: RVF as 5th EmbeddingProvider (52KB WASM replaces 540MB agentic-flow for basic use cases). Updated auto-select hierarchy: rvf > agentic-flow > transformers > mock. - Section 3B: ruvLLM learning persistence via RVF segments. SONA patterns (ReasoningBank), LoRA adapters, and EWC++ weights now survive process restarts. RuVectorProvider.searchMemory works offline via RVF without HTTP server. - Section 10: Progressive download architecture. Replaces npm optionalDependencies (all-or-nothing 1.3GB) with on-demand capability downloads stored as .rvf files. 5 phases from 22MB core CLI to full LLM/CNN/GNN/kernel capabilities. Implementation plan expanded from 5 to 8 phases. Testing strategy updated with 11 new test cases covering the additions. Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net> * feat: add optimized Docker images for Ruflo lite/full/workers - Dockerfile: Multi-stage Alpine build with aggressive dependency pruning. Result: 324MB image, 0.6s startup (vs 1.3GB/35s baseline). Uses --omit=optional + explicit rm -rf of heavy transitive deps. - Dockerfile.full: Full image variant with all optional dependencies including native compilation (onnxruntime-node, better-sqlite3). - Dockerfile.headless: Updated from node:20 to node:22, claude-flow branding to ruflo, same prune strategy as lite. - docker-compose.yml: Three profiles (lite, full, workers) with appropriate resource limits and health checks. - .dockerignore: Excludes node_modules, tests, source maps, IDE configs, and secrets from build context. Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net> * feat(ADR-057): implement RVF native storage backend across all packages Replace sql.js (18MB WASM) with zero-dependency RVF format across memory, events, embeddings, and learning subsystems. Pure TypeScript fallback with optional native @ruvector/rvf acceleration. New files: - memory/rvf-backend.ts: IMemoryBackend impl with HNSW + binary persistence - memory/hnsw-lite.ts: Lightweight HNSW graph for vector search - memory/rvf-learning-store.ts: SONA pattern/LoRA/EWC/trajectory persistence - memory/persistent-sona.ts: PersistentSonaCoordinator wrapping learning store - shared/events/rvf-event-log.ts: Append-only event log (replaces EventStore) - embeddings/rvf-embedding-cache.ts: Binary file embedding cache (FNV-1a keys) - embeddings/rvf-embedding-service.ts: Hash-based embedding provider (<0.1ms) Integrations: - DatabaseProvider: 'rvf' added, auto-selected first (always available) - EmbeddingProvider: 'rvf' added, auto-selected first in async factory - All packages export new RVF types and classes Tests: 163 passing (5 suites), validated in Docker (node:22-alpine) - rvf-backend: 50 tests (CRUD, query, search, persistence, HNSW) - rvf-event-log: 33 tests (append, replay, snapshots, crash recovery) - rvf-embeddings: 30 tests (cache, service, determinism, normalization) - rvf-learning-store: 31 tests (patterns, LoRA, EWC, trajectories) - rvf-integration: 19 tests (provider selection, migration, concurrency) Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net> * fix(ADR-057): harden RVF storage with security, performance, and migration Security: atomic writes (tmp+rename) across all backends, path validation, input validation at boundaries. Performance: persist lock prevents concurrent flushes, stack overflow fix for large datasets, timer.unref() prevents hangs, O(1) eviction lookup in embedding cache. New: bidirectional RVF migration utility (JSON/SQLite/RVF), format v2 with backward compat, auto-persist patterns and trajectories before eviction. Removes stale npx cache scanning that could report wrong versions. Fixes #1235. init: Remove invalid TeammateIdle and TaskCompleted keys from generated .claude/settings.json hooks section. | [.gitignore](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/.gitignore ".gitignore") | [.gitignore](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/.gitignore ".gitignore") | [security: untrack .env files + broaden .gitignore to block .env.*](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/commit/f8f4cd4bc754aeacb34a6bf11549fc7a81174e37 "security: untrack .env files + broaden .gitignore to block .env.* Untracks 3 .env files that were committed historically: - ruflo/src/ruvocal/.env (194 lines — chat-ui config, admin tokens, DB URL, etc.) - ruflo/src/ruvocal/.env.ci (MONGODB_URL) - v3/goal_ui/.env (Supabase project ID + URL + anon publishable key) Files remain on disk for local development; only removed from the index. Gitignore now matches `.env.*` broadly (was only `.env`, `.env.local`, `.env.*.local`) with an explicit `!.env.example` exception so templates stay trackable. Note: this is a non-destructive untrack. Historical commits still contain the values; rotation of any actual secrets is required. History rewrite was considered but declined — 4,655 forks retain independent copies regardless of canonical-history rewrite, and 50 open PRs would all need rebasing. Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net>") | May 5, 2026 |
| [.npmignore](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/.npmignore ".npmignore") | [.npmignore](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/.npmignore ".npmignore") | [chore(repo): archive legacy v2/ codebase under archive/v2/](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/commit/003ce127b57ac417dd434870c24b1ce64483fdcd "chore(repo): archive legacy v2/ codebase under archive/v2/ The pre-split claude-flow@2.x line is no longer built, tested, or published from this repo — v3/@claude-flow/* is the active codebase. Moving v2/ under archive/v2/ to make the inactive status visible from the directory layout while preserving full git history (6,440 renames, no content changes). - git mv v2 archive/v2 - new archive/README.md explains what's frozen, where the v3 equivalents live (cli, mcp-tools, hooks, memory), and a \"don't add new code here\" note - .npmignore: replace `v2/` rule with `archive/` so future archived items inherit the same exclusion automatically Auditing for downstream impact: no active package.json, tsconfig, or CI workflow imports from v2/. The only top-level reference was the .npmignore rule (now updated). Forks pinned to claude-flow@2.x can still reach the source via the git history regardless of folder name. Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net>") | May 6, 2026 |

| [LICENSE](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/LICENSE "LICENSE") | [LICENSE](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/LICENSE "LICENSE") | [fix(memory): support namespace in delete command (](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/commit/c38d56b60eb4c8b2a023e0645d37370cf977c0da "fix(memory): support namespace in delete command (#981) * fix(memory): add missing 'type' column to schema migration Fixes #977 - Memory store command failed with \"table memory_entries has no column named type\" on older databases that were missing this column. Changes: - Add 'type' column to ensureSchemaColumns() migration list - Fix .gitignore to use rooted paths (/memory/ instead of memory/) to prevent accidentally ignoring src/memory/ source code The ensureSchemaColumns() function now properly adds the 'type' column when upgrading databases from older schema versions. Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net> * chore: bump version to 3.0.0-alpha.153 Published to npm with fix for issue #977. Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net> * fix(memory): support namespace in delete command Fixes #980 - CLI memory delete now properly supports namespaced entries. Changes: - Add `deleteEntry()` function to memory-initializer.ts - Add `--key` and `--namespace` options to delete command - Support both positional argument and flag for key - Use consistent pattern with retrieve command Usage: memory delete --key \"mykey\" --namespace \"myns\" -f memory delete mykey -f (uses default namespace) Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net> * Checkpoint: File edits Automatic checkpoint created by Claude Code - Branch: fix/issue-980-memory-delete-namespace - Timestamp: 2026-01-22T15:55:06Z - Changes: 1 file(s) 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com> * fix(ci): update pnpm-lock.yaml for @claude-flow/browser Updates the lockfile to include dependencies for the browser package that were causing CI failures with frozen-lockfile mode. | [README.md](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/README.md "README.md") | [README.md](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/README.md "README.md") | [docs(readme): refresh top-of-readme badge cluster](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/commit/6af8ef10062f93d2b506136da7065f913589f792 "docs(readme): refresh top-of-readme badge cluster Three coordinated badge updates to the cluster between the project-meta and the H1: - new \"Codex Plugin\" badge linking to npmjs.com/package/@claude-flow/codex, OpenAI brand purple #412991. Shields.io silently dropped `logo=openai` after simple-icons removed it for trademark reasons, so the OpenAI spirograph mark is embedded directly via `logo=data:image/svg+xml;base64,…` (verified shields.io renders an <image> element with the logo). - new \"RuVector Agentic DB\" badge linking to github.com/ruvnet/ruvector, cyan #06b6d4 with the graphql logo (graph-themed mark for a graph-RAG database). - \"Claude Code\" badge: green → Anthropic brand orange #D97757, plus `logoColor=white` so the anthropic mark stays legible on the warmer background. Brings the badge in line with anthropic.com's identity instead of using a generic Bootstrap green. The cluster now reads top-to-bottom as: project meta (stars, license) → AI platform plugins (Claude Code, Codex) → underlying graph DB tech (RuVector). Three semantic groups, clear hierarchy, no emoji noise in the visible badge text. Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net>") | May 6, 2026 |
| [SECURITY.md](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/SECURITY.md "SECURITY.md") | [SECURITY.md](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/SECURITY.md "SECURITY.md") | [docs: update SECURITY.md contact email](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/commit/35e094aa8925d560a87bb1d126a198fb811753e4 "docs: update SECURITY.md contact email Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>") | Mar 19, 2026 |
| [package-lock.json](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/package-lock.json "package-lock.json") | [package-lock.json](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/package-lock.json "package-lock.json") | [fix(release): add missing cli runtime deps to umbrella package.json (c…](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/commit/8221903b0296663f6884a31154068c3ea4151375 "fix(release): add missing cli runtime deps to umbrella package.json (closes #1795) (#1797) * fix(release): add missing cli runtime deps to umbrella package.json The published `claude-flow` umbrella tarball bundles `v3/@claude-flow/cli/dist/**` and ships it as the runtime CLI, but the umbrella's `dependencies` only declared `semver` and `zod`. This caused a fatal `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` on first run for users who installed via `npx claude-flow@latest`: Cannot find package '@claude-flow/cli-core' imported from .../v3/@claude-flow/cli/dist/src/output.js Add the required runtime deps that the bundled cli dist imports (mirrored from `v3/@claude-flow/cli/package.json`): - @claude-flow/cli-core ^3.7.0-alpha.5 - @claude-flow/mcp ^3.0.0-alpha.8 - @claude-flow/neural ^3.0.0-alpha.8 - @claude-flow/shared ^3.0.0-alpha.7 - @noble/ed25519 ^2.1.0 - @ruvector/rabitq-wasm ^0.1.0 Bump umbrella version 3.7.0-alpha.8 -> 3.7.0-alpha.9. Verified locally on Windows: npx claude-flow@latest --version and --help both run cleanly with no module-resolution errors. Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net> * chore(lockfile): regenerate package-lock for added cli runtime deps Adds entries for the six runtime deps introduced in the previous commit so `npm ci` no longer fails with EUSAGE in CI. Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net>") | May 6, 2026 |

| [ruflo-plugins.gif](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/ruflo-plugins.gif "ruflo-plugins.gif") | [ruflo-plugins.gif](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/ruflo-plugins.gif "ruflo-plugins.gif") | [fix: rename plugin commands to avoid built-in conflicts; bump default…](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/commit/1405eab669261759e4346d83a4c272bdedc94e23 "fix: rename plugin commands to avoid built-in conflicts; bump default Opus to 4.7 (#1678) * chore: bump all packages to v3.6.11 — published to npm SmartRetrieval integration + 4 bug fixes (#1604, #1605, #1606, #1616). Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net> * fix: rename plugin commands to avoid built-in conflicts; bump default Opus model - Rename 9 plugin commands/skills whose bare names collided with built-in Claude Code slash commands and skills (/doctor, /loop, /schedule, /browser, /status, /cost, /memory, /docs, /sparc) to ruflo-* prefixed names - Update ruflo init to default to claude-opus-4-7 (was 4.6) in settings.json modelPreferences and statusline display - Update statusline pkgVersion fallback 3.5 -> 3.6 to match published version - Add plugin showcase GIF above Quick Start in README Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net> --------- Co-authored-by: Reuven <cohen@ruv-mac-mini.local>") | Apr 30, 2026 |
| [tsconfig.json](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/tsconfig.json "tsconfig.json") | [tsconfig.json](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/tsconfig.json "tsconfig.json") | [checkpoint: File edit:](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/commit/c00fc99a828c0f29f2e3615fb5f7be82e611c2ef "checkpoint: File edit: 🚀 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) 📊 V3 Development Progress Checkpoint Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4 <noreply@anthropic.com>") | Jan 4, 2026 |
| [verification-inventory.json](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/verification-inventory.json "verification-inventory.json") | [verification-inventory.json](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/verification-inventory.json "verification-inventory.json") | [chore(verification): regenerate inventory + fix CRLF bug in extractor](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/commit/3abfb5d0aa255de87003e00a444a978b8b5355d7 "chore(verification): regenerate inventory + fix CRLF bug in extractor Audit (via goal-planner agent) flagged 6 stale entries: 5 new MCP tools in browser-session-tools.ts + 1 new agent (dossier-investigator in ruflo-goals). Counts now 305 MCP / 49 CLI / 32 plugins / 44 agents. Also fixes a latent bug in scripts/inventory-capabilities.mjs that extractAgents() never extracted anything on Windows checkouts because the frontmatter regex `/^---\n([\s\S]*?)\n---/` requires LF. On a CRLF working tree it returns 0 agents while silently treating the result as authoritative. Made the regex CRLF-tolerant (`/^---\r?\n([\s\S]*?)\r?\n---/`) so the script is platform-portable; this is what unblocked today's regen on a Windows machine. Three files updated in lockstep: - scripts/inventory-capabilities.mjs — CRLF-tolerant frontmatter - verification-inventory.json — re-extracted, sorted, deterministic - verification.md — narrative coverage line + the full embedded markdown tables (lines 410+) regenerated from the same run. The signed JSON witness manifest above (lines 70-317, Ed25519 sig at line 313) is untouched, so the cryptographic signature remains valid. The cli-core split (#1764) created v3/@claude-flow/cli-core/src/mcp-tools/ with 13 hooks_*/memory_* defs, but every name there is a duplicate of what the umbrella's hooks-tools.ts / memory-tools.ts already register — no new MCP surface, so the script's hard-coded MCP_TOOLS_DIR pointing at v3/@claude-flow/cli/src/mcp-tools is currently correct. If cli-core ever ships a unique tool, the script will silently miss it; worth a follow-up to scan both dirs and de-duplicate. Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net>") | May 6, 2026 |

The signed JSON witness manifest above (lines 70-317, Ed25519 sig at line 313) is untouched, so the cryptographic signature remains valid. The cli-core split (#1764) created v3/@claude-flow/cli-core/src/mcp-tools/ with 13 hooks_*/memory_* defs, but every name there is a duplicate of what the umbrella's hooks-tools.ts / memory-tools.ts already register — no new MCP surface, so the script's hard-coded MCP_TOOLS_DIR pointing at v3/@claude-flow/cli/src/mcp-tools is currently correct. If cli-core ever ships a unique tool, the script will silently miss it; worth a follow-up to scan both dirs and de-duplicate. Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net>") | May 6, 2026 |
| [verification.md.json](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/verification.md.json "verification.md.json") | [verification.md.json](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/verification.md.json "verification.md.json") | [fix(init):](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/commit/229d273a1f7578b8158f8778126f8128a845276f "fix(init): #1744 honest defaults + runtime fallbacks (v3.6.28) (#1753) * fix(init): #1744 honest defaults — --no-global, --minimal hooks gate, README install paths (v3.6.28) Three fixes from @ruvnet's install-study (issue #1744) plus three already-on-disk runtime honesty fixes: #1744 changes (this commit): 1. **--no-global flag** for `init` (and codex/dual variants) — opt out of the user-global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md \"Ruflo Integration\" pointer block append. Default behavior unchanged (block still appended once, idempotent — but now opt-out is documented). #1744 #2. 2. **--minimal hooks gate** in settings-generator. Before this commit, `--minimal` wrote .claude/settings.json with 4 hook decls pointing at .claude/helpers/hook-handler.cjs but didn't install the helpers directory — so every hook fired and silently failed. Now the hooks block only emits when components.helpers is true. Minimal stays minimal AND functional. #1744 #3. 3. **README Quick Start labeling** — added a head-to-head table distinguishing Plugin install (lite, slash commands only, no MCP) from CLI install (full Ruflo loop, MCP registered, hooks installed). The previous README marketed both equally as \"Recommended\" which surprised users who chose plugin install and found the MCP tools missing. #1744 #1. Already-on-disk runtime honesty fixes (bundled in this release): 4. v3/@claude-flow/cli/src/commands/performance.ts — drop unverified \"Flash Attention 2.49x speedup\" optimize/bottleneck recommendations. Re-tagged as \"in progress, currently JS reference impl\". 5. v3/@claude-flow/cli/src/mcp-tools/neural-tools.ts — drop the silent Tier 4 mock-fallback in embedding resolution. If Tier 1 (agentic-flow) and Tier 3 (ONNX) both failed to import, leave realEmbeddings null and let the explicit hash-fallback path run with a clear _embeddingNote in stats. Silently substituting mock embeddings hid a missing production dependency from callers. 6. v3/@claude-flow/embeddings/src/embedding-service.ts — throw on unknown provider / no-real-provider-available paths instead of silently substituting MockEmbeddingService. Mock embeddings are tests-only; production code that lands on the mock path accidentally is a bug, and silent fallback hid it. All TypeScript clean (tsc --noEmit). Build verified — all 3 fixes present in dist/. Closes #1744 (3 of 5 papercuts; remaining 2 are doc-only, addressed in PR #1751). Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net> * chore(release): bump to 3.6.28 — published to npm @claude-flow/cli@3.6.28 → tags: latest, alpha, v3alpha claude-flow@3.6.28 → tags: latest, alpha, v3alpha ruflo@3.6.28 → tags: latest, alpha, v3alpha Includes: #1744 (--no-global, --minimal hooks gate, README install labeling) + runtime honesty fixes (drop unverified Flash Attention claim, drop silent mock embedding fallback, throw on missing real embedding provider). Co-Authored-By: RuFlo <ruv@ruv.net> * chore(release): regen verification witness for 3.6.28 totalFixes: 27 → 55 (adds CAP-MCP capability inventory: 300 tools across 28 source files) releases: 3.6.24 → 3.6.28 (current published) manifestHash + signature regenerated from current git commit Includes the #1744 + runtime-honesty fixes shipped in 3.6.28. Run `node scripts/regenerate-witness.mjs` after each release. # Ruflo

[](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo#ruflo)

**Multi-agent AI orchestration for Claude Code**

Orchestrate 100+ specialized AI agents across machines, teams, and trust boundaries. Ruflo adds coordinated swarms, self-learning memory, federated comms, and enterprise security to Claude Code — so agents don't just run, they collaborate.

### Why Ruflo?

[](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo#why-ruflo)

> Claude Flow is now Ruflo — named by [`rUv`](https://ruv.io/), who loves Rust, flow states, and building things that feel inevitable. The "Ru" is the rUv. The "flo" is working until 3am. Underneath, powered by [`Cognitum.One`](https://cognitum.one/?RuFlo) agentic architecture, running a supercharged Rust based AI engine, embeddings, memory, and plugin system.

### What Ruflo Does

[](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo#what-ruflo-does)

One `npx ruvflo init` gives Claude Code a nervous system: agents self-organize into swarms, learn from every task, remember across sessions, and — with federation — securely talk to agents on other machines without leaking data. You keep writing code. Ruflo handles the coordination.

```
Self-Learning / Self-Optimizing Agent Architecture

User --> Ruflo (CLI/MCP) --> Router --> Swarm --> Agents --> Memory --> LLM Providers
                          ^                           |
                          +---- Learning Loop <-------+
```

> **New to Ruflo?** You don't need to learn 314 MCP tools or 26 CLI commands. After `init`, just use Claude Code normally -- the hooks system automatically routes tasks, learns from successful patterns, and coordinates agents in the background.

[![Image 23: Ruflo Plugins](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/raw/main/ruflo-plugins.gif)](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/ruflo-plugins.gif)[![Image 24: Ruflo Plugins](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/raw/main/ruflo-plugins.gif)](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/ruflo-plugins.gif)[](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo/blob/main/ruflo-plugins.gif)

## Quick Start

[](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo#quick-start)

There are **two different install paths** with very different surface areas. Pick based on what you need (#1744):

|  | **Claude Code Plugin** | **CLI install (`npx ruflo init`)** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| What it gives you | Slash commands + a few skills + agent definitions per-plugin | Full Ruflo loop — 98 agents, 60+ commands, 30 skills, MCP server, hooks, daemon |
| Files in your workspace | **Zero** | `.claude/`, `.claude-flow/`, `CLAUDE.md`, helpers, settings |
| MCP server registered | **No** (`memory_store`, `swarm_init`, etc. unavailable to Claude) | Yes |
| Hooks installed | No | Yes |
| Best for | Try a single plugin's commands without committing to the full install | Production use — everything works as documented |

### Path A — Claude Code Plugins (lite, slash commands only)

[](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo#path-a--claude-code-plugins-lite-slash-commands-only)

undefinedshell
# Add the marketplace
/plugin marketplace add ruvnet/ruflo

# Install core + any plugins you need
/plugin install ruflo-core@ruflo
/plugin install ruflo-swarm@ruflo
/plugin install ruflo-autopilot@ruflo
/plugin install ruflo-federation@ruflo
undefined

This adds slash commands and agent definitions only. The Ruflo MCP server is NOT registered, so `memory_store`, `swarm_init`, `agent_spawn`, etc. won't be callable from Claude. For the full loop, use Path B below.

### CLI Install

[](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo#cli-install)

undefinedshell
# One-line install
curl -fsSL https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/ruvnet/ruflo@main/scripts/install.sh | bash

# Or via npx (interactive setup)
npx ruflo@latest init wizard

# Quick non-interactive init
# npx ruflo@latest init

# Or install globally
npm install -g ruflo@latest
undefined

### MCP Server

[](https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo#mcp-server)

undefinedshell
# Add Ruflo as an MCP server in Claude Code (canonical form, matches USERGUIDE.md)
claude mcp add ruflo -- npx ruflo@latest mcp start
undefined
